procedure: Excel sheet is being maintained by IT Executive for keeping track of IT assets. All relevant information related to IT assets (hardware and software) is updated in the excel sheet including warranty dates, AMC dates, and depreciation information.
control: To ensure accurate and automated tracking of IT assets to prevent loss, unauthorized use, and ensure timely maintenance.

procedure: Regular physical verification of IT assets is conducted quarterly by the IT team to reconcile records with actual inventory.
control: Quarterly physical verification ensures that all assets are accounted for and records are accurate.

procedure: All new employees must request access through the HR ticketing system. IT department reviews and approves access based on role requirements. Access is provisioned within 24 hours of approval.
control: Role-based access control ensures that employees only have access to resources necessary for their job functions.

procedure: Access rights are reviewed quarterly by department heads. Any unused or excessive privileges are revoked immediately. IT sends automated reminders 2 weeks before review deadlines.
control: Periodic access reviews prevent privilege creep and ensure principle of least privilege.

procedure: Security incidents must be reported immediately through the security hotline or incident@company.com. Initial triage is performed within 30 minutes. Severity classification: Critical (immediate), High (2 hours), Medium (24 hours), Low (1 week).
control: Rapid incident detection and response minimizes impact and prevents escalation.

procedure: Post-incident reviews are conducted within 5 business days. Root cause analysis document must be completed and lessons learned shared with relevant teams. Corrective actions are tracked in Jira.
control: Post-incident analysis ensures continuous improvement and prevents recurrence.

procedure: Critical databases are backed up daily at 2 AM EST. Full backups on Sundays, incremental backups Monday-Saturday. Backup success is verified through automated monitoring. Failed backups trigger immediate alerts to on-call engineer.
control: Regular automated backups ensure business continuity and data recovery capability.

procedure: Disaster recovery drills are conducted quarterly. Recovery Time Objective (RTO) is 4 hours, Recovery Point Objective (RPO) is 24 hours. All drills are documented with success criteria and lessons learned.
control: Regular DR testing validates recovery procedures and identifies gaps.

procedure: All new vendors must complete security questionnaire before contract signing. High-risk vendors (handling sensitive data) undergo detailed security audit. Re-assessments are performed annually.
control: Vendor security assessments ensure third-party risks are identified and mitigated.

procedure: Passwords must be minimum 12 characters with uppercase, lowercase, numbers, and special characters. Password expiry is 90 days. Password history prevents reuse of last 10 passwords. Multi-factor authentication is mandatory for privileged accounts.
control: Strong password requirements prevent unauthorized access through credential compromise.

procedure: All production changes require CAB (Change Advisory Board) approval. Emergency changes require verbal approval from CTO followed by post-implementation documentation. Change windows are Tuesday/Thursday 10 PM - 2 AM EST.
control: Structured change management reduces production incidents and ensures proper testing.

procedure: Data is classified as: Public, Internal, Confidential, or Restricted. Restricted data requires encryption at rest and in transit. Data classification labels must be applied to all documents and systems.
control: Data classification ensures appropriate security controls are applied based on sensitivity.
